brief_summary: Three amber-bordered cards naming the under-specified variables behind every 'gasket mount' label. Card 1: plate material with four bars (PC, POM, FR4, brass) growing wider as stiffness increases. Card 2: gasket durometer × thickness with thick-40A vs thin-70A rectangles indicating deflection range. Card 3: attachment count with a 4-dot and an 8-dot plate diagram side by side. Caption: 'two boards, identical gasket-mount label — three variables apart'.
data_sources: gasket-mount-reality.mdx § 'The three variables that determine the result' — 'Plate material ... A PC plate on a gasket mount produces ... a deep, slightly muted thock ... A brass plate on the same mount produces a completely different result — tighter, more articulate, heavier ... Gasket hardness and thickness. Silicone gaskets have a Shore A durometer rating. A 40A gasket gives substantially more than a 70A gasket ... Attachment point count. A board with four gasket pads ... A board with eight or ten contact points limits lateral movement, stiffens the effective deflection ... This is the variable that low-cost gasket-mount designs compress on first'
